stem: What is the legal minimum tyre tread depth on a passenger car in the Netherlands?

choices.text: 0.8 mm
1.6 mm
3.0 mm
4.0 mm

explanation.short: The legal minimum is 1.6 mm across the central three-quarters of the tyre.
explanation.examTip: For winter or wet conditions, tread closer to 3–4 mm is much safer.

choices.text: Only at the APK inspection
Only when tyres are hot from driving
At least once a month, with tyres cold
Once a year at the dealer

explanation.short: Check pressure cold, at least once a month, using the value on the door pillar.
explanation.examTip: Correct pressure also reduces fuel consumption.

stem: Your car has ABS. In an emergency stop you should:

choices.text: Pump the pedal repeatedly
Press the pedal firmly and keep steering
Press the pedal lightly to avoid skidding
Release the brake every time you feel a pulse

explanation.short: With ABS, press hard and steer — the system stops the wheels locking.
explanation.examTip: The pulsing you feel in the pedal is normal ABS operation, not a fault.

stem: When may you use your rear fog light?

choices.text: Whenever it is dark
In rain at night
Only when visibility is reduced to less than about 50 metres
Whenever you are on a motorway

explanation.short: Rear fog lights are only for severely reduced visibility (under about 50 m).
explanation.examTip: Switch them off as soon as visibility improves; they dazzle drivers behind you.

stem: Hazard warning lights may be used to:

choices.text: Park briefly where parking is forbidden
Warn following traffic of a sudden traffic jam on the motorway
Indicate that you are turning
Show that you are driving fast

explanation.short: Use hazard lights to warn of an unexpected danger such as a sudden jam.
explanation.examTip: They do not give permission to park where parking is otherwise forbidden.

stem: On a frosty winter morning, before driving you must:

choices.text: Clear only a peephole in the windscreen
Clear all windows, mirrors, lights and number plates of snow and ice
Pour hot water over the windscreen
Start driving and let the wipers clear it

explanation.short: All windows, mirrors, lights and plates must be clear before you set off.
explanation.examTip: Snow on the roof can also slide onto your windscreen or onto traffic behind you.

stem: Who is responsible for ensuring a 12-year-old passenger wears their seat belt?

choices.text: The passenger themselves
The driver
Nobody — at that age it is optional
The owner of the car, even if not driving

explanation.short: The driver is responsible for all passengers under 18 wearing seat belts.
explanation.examTip: Passengers aged 18 or older are responsible for themselves.

stem: You want to put a baby in a rear-facing child seat on the front passenger seat. What must you check?

choices.text: That the seat belt is fitted
That the front passenger airbag is switched off
That the child is older than 6 months
Nothing — it is always allowed

explanation.short: A rear-facing child seat must never be used on a seat with an active front airbag.
explanation.examTip: Children under 1.35 m should normally travel in the back.

stem: You are carrying long planks that stick out behind the car. How far may the load overhang at the rear and what must you do?

choices.text: Up to 0.5 m, no marker needed
Up to 1 m, with a red marker at the end
Up to 2 m, no marker needed
No overhang is ever allowed

explanation.short: Rear overhang up to 1 m is allowed, marked with a red flag or marker board.
explanation.examTip: Lights and number plate must remain visible, and sideways overhang is not allowed.

stem: Which documents must you be able to show when driving in the Netherlands?

choices.text: Only your driving licence
Driving licence, vehicle registration and proof of insurance
Passport only
Driving licence and a recent fuel receipt

explanation.short: Licence, vehicle registration and proof of insurance must be available on request.
explanation.examTip: Road tax and APK status are checked from the registration record itself.

choices.text: A road tax payment
An insurance policy
The periodic vehicle inspection for roadworthiness
A licence for driving abroad

explanation.short: APK is the periodic technical inspection that confirms a vehicle is roadworthy.
explanation.examTip: Driving without a valid APK is an offence and can invalidate insurance cover.

stem: A circular sign marks the start of an environmental zone (milieuzone). What does this mean?

choices.text: All motor traffic is forbidden
Certain older or more polluting vehicles are not allowed in the zone
You must drive with hazard lights on
Free parking is available inside the zone

explanation.short: An environmental zone restricts the more polluting vehicles, especially older diesels.
explanation.examTip: Each city sets its own exact rules — check before driving into one.

stem: What is the minimum legal tread depth for a passenger-car tyre in the Netherlands?

choices.text: 1.0 mm
1.6 mm
2.5 mm
4.0 mm

explanation.short: Legal minimum is 1.6 mm across the central three-quarters of the tread.
explanation.examTip: Many manufacturers recommend replacement well above the legal minimum, around 3 mm, especially for winter tyres.

stem: When must you use dipped headlights instead of side lights only?

choices.text: Only at night
Whenever visibility is reduced — dusk, rain, fog or snow — and at night
Only on motorways
Only when other cars do

explanation.short: Use dipped headlights any time visibility is reduced; side lights alone are not enough for moving traffic.
explanation.examTip: Most modern cars switch on automatic daytime running lights — those are not the same as dipped beam at night.

stem: A child of 3 years old, height 95 cm, must travel in a private car. What is required?

choices.text: An adult seat-belt is enough
An approved child restraint suitable for the child's size and weight
The child must sit in the front seat
The child must travel on an adult's lap

explanation.short: Children under 1.35 m must use an approved child seat or booster suitable for their size and weight.
explanation.examTip: If the front airbag is active, never put a rear-facing seat in the front.

choices.text: An annual road-tax payment
The Dutch periodic vehicle inspection that must be passed for the car to be legal on the road
A driver's licence renewal exam
A type of car insurance

explanation.short: The APK (Algemene Periodieke Keuring) is a periodic safety and emissions check; without a valid APK you may not drive on public roads.
explanation.examTip: For most passenger cars APK is yearly once the car is 4 years old.
